label: llm
text:
the engineers at the site.
It concluded that a 15,000-tonne train was being pulled towards the track on an eastbound axle and a white smoke was being seen as it neared the track.
The train had not crossed the line and was about to make the turn, when the white smoke was reported to have travelled from the side of the train through a small hole in the track.
It was then felt that the train was going to hit another train on the opposite side of the line, causing a collision.
The train was stopped and the white smoke was removed. The train was then towed away.
The report said that engineers had not been warned of the train's approach, but had ignored the white smoke which they believed was a false alarm.
However, the Board said that the report was wrong and the accident must be attributed to "a fault in the approach of a train to a point of intersection".
The train was moving towards the line at a speed of 10mph (16km/h) when it was stopped, the report said.

AnÃ­bal Cavaco Silva said the budget didn't treat citizens fairly, and hit some of them worse than others.
The right-of-centre
label: llm
text:
Socialist leader said the budget cuts had been "tougher than expected", with the rest of the country "worse off".
Mr Cavaco Silva told the BBC's The World programme that the government's policy of cutting spending on education was "not sustainable".
He added that the country had "a huge debt" and called the government's plans to reduce spending on pensions and healthcare "a big mistake".
But he said the budget was "fairer" than the previous government, and that "it is about the future of the Portuguese people".
The right-of-centre candidate for the Portuguese Senate, whose party controls the lower house of parliament, said the Portuguese government was "doing the right thing".
"I'm happy that the Portuguese government is doing what is necessary for the future of the country," Mr Cavaco Silva told the programme.

Mr Cavaco Silva said he believed it was necessary to reduce the budget and reduce the debt "without compromising on the quality of public services" such as education.
